does: This paper identifies and addresses a specific failure mode called the snowball effect in safety alignment of large reasoning models, where minor reasoning deviations compound through multi-step chain-of-thought processes, resulting in either harmful outputs or excessive refusal. The authors propose AdvChain, an adversarial tuning method that constructs datasets with Temptation-Correction pairs (teaching recovery from harmful reasoning drifts) and Hesitation-Correction pairs (addressing unnecessary caution), enabling models to learn dynamic self-correction rather than merely imitating perfect reasoning scripts. Experimental results demonstrate that AdvChain substantially improves robustness against jailbreak attacks and chain-of-thought hijacking while maintaining model capability.
connects: This work builds on recent advances in aligning large language models through instruction tuning and chain-of-thought prompting, extending safety alignment methods beyond single-token prediction to multi-step reasoning processes. It responds to limitations in existing CoT safety tuning approaches that treat reasoning pathways as static sequences to imitate, drawing connections to broader literature on adversarial training for robustness and self-correction mechanisms in language models. The paper addresses emerging challenges specific to reasoning-capable systems, connecting to growing concerns about jailbreaking and prompt injection attacks on capable models while building on foundations of constitutional AI and preference learning methods.
enables: This work opens directions for more robust alignment of increasingly capable reasoning systems by establishing that self-correction during reasoning, rather than static adherence to templates, is crucial for safety. It enables future research into dynamic safety properties of multi-step inference, potentially informing how to align systems with longer or more complex reasoning horizons. The approach suggests pathways for developing reasoning models that gracefully handle adversarial inputs and maintain safety properties under distribution shift, laying groundwork for safer deployment of reasoning-capable systems in real-world applications where CoT hijacking and adversarial prompts pose genuine risks.
